title: MATLAB: Discrete-Time Signals and Systems
summary: This page explains how to use MATLAB's stem command to plot a discrete-time function over a finite integer index range and emphasizes that anonymous functions do not automatically enforce integer-valued indices. It also compares the discrete-time sequences f[−2n] and f[−2n+1], noting that unlike the continuous-time case they are not simple shifts of one another, and warns that MATLAB may not perform intended upsampling behavior without careful implementation.
